seoTitle: Port Alma vs Port Curtis — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Alma and Port Curtis. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Alma vs Port Curtis: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Alma and Port Curtis.
heading: Port Alma vs Port Curtis — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Alma, Queensland and Port Curtis, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Alma has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Port Curtis (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Alma or Port Curtis safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Alma has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Port Curtis scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Alma scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Port Curtis scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Alma or Port Curtis?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Alma has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
